title: Botox parties - good or bad？
excerpt: So botox parties good or bad [Music] well um this is a very contentious topic because obviously what you're actually saying from a healthcare practitioner's perspective is non-elective medical intervention parties um this doesn't really go well together you don't have a party and…

segments.text: so botox parties good or bad [Music] well um this is a very contentious topic because obviously what you're actually saying from a healthcare practitioner's perspective is non-elective medical intervention parties um this doesn't really go well together you don't have a party and then do uh medical procedures so there's instantly a clash now of course they do happen and the reason it's not just a snobbish thing that we say they're not a good idea the reason that they're difficult is because of patient autonomy being eroded as a healthcare practitioner we have a different standard we have to be protecting our patients decision-making process and so if you're going to do a procedure on someone you need them to have made that decision in a clear frame of mind without any external influences such as their best friend saying unicorn habits such as a glass of champagne that they've just downed you know that kind of giddy this is exciting feeling let's have some

botox or some dermal filler that is not a good way to make that decision so there's nothing wrong with getting people together and this happens as well all over the place you get people together do a demonstration and then take bookings if you're interested in doing that in terms of developing your practice um but the bare minimum is that you and your patient have a quiet moment in a room and do a consultation where they decide in a clear frame of mind without any alcohol in this system which is very important do they want to take this particular risk because that's really the key question they're weighing up benefits and risks there are some serious side effects as we know with all the procedures that we do and they're trying to make the decision about whether this is in their interest long term to take this risk now you cannot do that if you if you've had a bit to drink or you've got your best friends all kind of ex making you feel excited and you're you know maybe there's a special offer only on the night which is often the case like you can only get this procedure then and there or you'll never have it all of those things are eroding patient autonomy and so you really should build into your system a way that your patient can make the

decision independently in a calm state of mind and the best way to do that is to by all means do your demonstration evening then take bookings and come back and do the procedures when the patient is in a clear state of mind and you will have some of those patients cancelled and that's a good thing because it's better to have fewer people treated than to treat someone who didn't actually want the procedure ends up with a complication and then sues you so botox parties the way they're often done bad idea probably unethical probably against professional standards and but you will see them being done around the country and around the world because i know this still happened but you can do them in a way that gives the patient room and that's what i suggest that you do autonomy is key let them make the decision in a clear frame of mind hope that helps you
